name: use-focus-zone
title: useFocusZone
description: Manage focus across multiple zones with arrow key and Tab transitions. Supports controlled and uncontrolled zone state.

content.text: Options for multi-zone focus management and inter-zone keyboard transitions.
The initial active zone when in uncontrolled mode.
Controlled zone value. When provided, the hook operates in controlled mode.
Called whenever the active zone changes.
Called before focus leaves a zone.
Called when focus enters a zone.
Custom arrow-key transition function; return a target zone or null to block.
Zone order for Tab/Shift+Tab cycling; omitted means native Tab behavior.
Keyboard scope name to push while the focus zone is active; null skips registration.
Optional DOM subtree used to scope registered focus-zone keys.
When true, focus-zone keys only run while focus is inside containerRef.
Whether focus-zone keys may run when an input-like element has focus.
Default preventDefault behavior inherited by transition keys and key helpers.
Whether the focus zone hook is active.
Optional DOM focus targets for zone changes.
DOM attributes for a zone container.
Present when the zone is active.
Ref-like value that resolves to a zone container or target element.
* Focus target for a zone: a single ref/getter, or a container plus optional
* inner target that avoids focus repair while focus remains inside the container.
Container used for containment checks and focus-zone synchronization.
Specific element to focus when the zone becomes active.
Focus target configuration keyed by zone name.
Whether the initial zone should receive focus on mount.
Pass `preventScroll` when moving focus to a zone target.
Returns true if the current zone matches any provided zone.
Returns `UseKeyOptions` scoped to one zone, merged with optional extras.
Returns DOM attributes for a zone's container element.
* Models a layout as named zones, routes arrow or Tab transitions between them,
* and optionally moves DOM focus to the active zone target.
Current zone is not part of the cycle; Tab enters the cycle from either end.
Resolve every container that anchors this focus zone (per-zone focus targets
plus an explicit containerRef). Returns null when no containment is
resolvable, in which case Tab keeps its legacy document-wide cycle.
Outside every container: decline so native Tab proceeds (no preventDefault).
The listener set is reconciled when the resolvable document set actually
changes (e.g. a cross-document target mounts late). A keystroke never changes
that set, so the listener is not churned per keypress; per-event container

docs.description: Manage focus across multiple zones with arrow key and Tab transitions. Supports controlled and uncontrolled zone state.

parameters.name: initial
parameters.description: The initial active zone when in uncontrolled mode.

parameters.name: zones
parameters.description: All available zone identifiers.

parameters.name: zone
parameters.description: Controlled zone value. When provided, the hook operates in controlled mode.

parameters.name: onZoneChange
parameters.description: Called whenever the active zone changes.

parameters.name: onLeaveZone
parameters.description: Called when focus leaves a zone.

parameters.name: onEnterZone
parameters.description: Called when focus enters a zone.

parameters.name: transitions
parameters.description: Custom transition function. Return the target zone for a key press, or null to block the transition.

parameters.name: tabCycle
parameters.description: Zone order for Tab/Shift+Tab cycling. When omitted, Tab/Shift+Tab are not handled and follow native browser behavior.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Keyboard scope name to push while the focus zone is active. Null skips scope registration.

parameters.name: containerRef
parameters.description: Optional DOM subtree used to scope registered focus-zone keys.

parameters.name: focusWithinOnly
parameters.description: When true, focus-zone keys only run while focus is inside containerRef.

parameters.name: allowInInput
parameters.description: Whether focus-zone keys may run when an input-like element has focus.

parameters.name: preventDefault
parameters.description: Default preventDefault behavior inherited by transition keys and getKeyOptions helpers.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Whether the focus zone hook is active.

parameters.name: focus
parameters.type: { targets: Partial<Record<T, FocusZoneTarget>>; autoFocus?: boolean; preventScroll?: boolean }
parameters.description: Optional DOM focus targets for zone changes. A target may be a ref/function, or a { container, target } pair that skips focus repair while focus is already inside the container. Initial mount does not focus unless autoFocus is true.

returns.description: Object with zone state and helpers for conditional rendering and key options.

properties.name: isZone
properties.description: Returns true if the current zone matches any of the provided zones.

properties.name: getKeyOptions
properties.description: Returns UseKeyOptions scoped to a specific zone, merging with optional extra options.

properties.name: getZoneProps
properties.description: Returns DOM attributes for a zone's container element. Sets data-focused when the zone is active.

notes.title: Generic over zone type
notes.content: useFocusZone is generic over T extends string. Use `as const` arrays for full type inference of zone names.

notes.title: Controlled and uncontrolled
notes.content: Pass zone + onZoneChange for controlled mode, or rely on initial for uncontrolled mode.

notes.title: Scoped DOM handling
notes.content: Use containerRef + focusWithinOnly when multiple keyboard regions share a page-level scope.

notes.title: Requires KeyboardProvider
notes.content: useFocusZone registers key bindings through the provider context and must be used within a <KeyboardProvider> tree.

content.text: biome-ignore lint/a11y/useFocusableInteractive: WAI-ARIA listbox pattern — options stay non-focusable; the listbox container holds focus and active state is tracked via aria-selected.
Tab / Shift+Tab cycle through the three regions in order.
Tab and Shift+Tab cycle Search → Results → Preview. Active zone:
